Low.insight: Psychological Health & Safety is currently generating friction and variability. In Psychological Safety terms, this indicates the fundamentals are not yet consistently experienced across teams. Priority is to rebuild confidence by strengthening Awareness/Desire and demonstrating follow‑through.
Low.modelDescription: This may indicate limited psychological safety, where individuals hesitate to speak up, raise concerns, or challenge ideas openly.
Low.coachingTips:
STABILIZE & REBUILD: Psychological Health & Safety
Psychological Safety focus — restore trust, speak‑up and constructive conflict habits.

ADKAR Focus (Awareness/Desire trigger):
• Awareness: run a confidential speak‑up pulse to quantify where silence is occurring.
• Desire: senior leaders commit publicly to ‘raise issues early’ and protect candor.
• Knowledge: equip leaders with micro‑behaviours that build safety and close loops.
• Ability: implement a weekly issues‑to‑action ritual with owners and timelines.
• Reinforcement: publish quarterly ‘what we heard / what we changed’ updates.
Low.recommendedPrograms:
Psychological Health & Safety – Recommended OCM Tactics:
• Leadership Trust Reset
• Speak‑Up Pulse + Close‑Loop Plan
• EI & Coaching Micro‑Skills Lab
• Issues‑to‑Action Ritual Sprint
• Safety/Trust KPIs in QBR

High.insight: Psychological Health & Safety is a relative strength and can be leveraged as a model. Focus shifts to Reinforcement—codify what works, scale it, and protect momentum through governance and recognition.
High.modelDescription: This reflects a culture where individuals feel safe to contribute ideas, voice concerns, and engage in healthy dialogue without fear of negative consequences.
High.coachingTips:
SCALE & INSTITUTIONALIZE: Psychological Health & Safety
Psychological Safety focus — institutionalize a high‑trust learning culture.

ADKAR Focus (Reinforcement trigger):
• Awareness/Desire: sustain executive modeling in visible forums and onboarding.
• Knowledge: codify safety rituals into playbooks and leader standards.
• Ability: replicate practices enterprise‑wide with coaching support.
• Reinforcement: shift to leading indicators (innovation rate, learning cycles).
High.recommendedPrograms:
Psychological Health & Safety – Recommended OCM Tactics:
• Enterprise Safety/Trust Playbook
• Leader Onboarding Module
• People Leader Community of Practice
• Culture Ambassador Network
• Leading Indicator Dashboard

Medium.insight: Mindset & Adaptability is developing, but consistency varies by leader/team. The constraint is Knowledge/Ability—capability and routines are not yet standardized. Focus now is to translate intent into repeatable practice.

ADKAR Focus (Knowledge/Ability trigger):
• Awareness: clarify what’s changing vs. stable to reduce uncertainty.
• Desire: empower managers to protect focus and reduce churn.
• Knowledge: enable leaders with change planning tools.
• Ability: standardize readiness checks and change routines.
• Reinforcement: retire low‑value work quarterly.
Medium.recommendedPrograms:
Mindset & Adaptability – Recommended OCM Tactics:
• Coaching Conversations Mastery
• Peer Feedback Circles
• Standard Retrospectives (AARs)
• Manager Sponsor Enablement
• Quarterly Safety Pulse

Operational Discipline focus — tighten operating rhythms and performance routines.

ADKAR Focus (Knowledge/Ability trigger):
• Awareness: reinforce decision discipline and meeting purpose.
• Desire: strengthen leader participation and ownership.
• Knowledge: improve meeting hygiene and decision documentation.
• Ability: standardize operating rhythm across functions.
• Reinforcement: quarterly governance effectiveness review.
Medium.recommendedPrograms:
Workflow Clarity – Recommended OCM Tactics:
• Governance Effectiveness Review
• Standard Operating Rhythm Rollout
• Kaizen Improvement Sprints
• Commitment Dashboard
• Quarterly Performance Calibration

High.insight: Workflow Clarity is a relative strength and can be leveraged as a model. Focus shifts to Reinforcement—codify what works, scale it, and protect momentum through governance and recognition.
High.modelDescription: At the employee level, this reflects lived workplace experience, team climate, and individual nervous system responses to workload and leadership signals. This reflects clearly defined processes, decision pathways, and cross-functional coordination.
High.coachingTips:
SCALE & INSTITUTIONALIZE: Workflow Clarity
Operational Discipline focus — optimize throughput, quality and decision speed at scale.

ADKAR Focus (Reinforcement trigger):
• Awareness/Desire: keep governance disciplined as complexity grows.
• Knowledge: codify governance playbooks.
• Ability: scale governance practices to new portfolios.
• Reinforcement: optimize decision speed and quality.
High.recommendedPrograms:
Workflow Clarity – Recommended OCM Tactics:
• Governance/Execution Playbooks
• Cross‑Team Scaling Initiative
• Decision Speed/Quality Optimization
• Operational Excellence Program
• Enterprise Recognition

Low.insight: Workflow Clarity is currently generating friction and variability. In Operational Discipline terms, this indicates the fundamentals are not yet consistently experienced across teams. Priority is to rebuild confidence by strengthening Awareness/Desire and demonstrating follow‑through.
Low.modelDescription: At the employee level, this reflects lived workplace experience, team climate, and individual nervous system responses to workload and leadership signals. This may indicate ambiguity in roles, decision rights, or cross-functional processes, creating bottlenecks or duplication.

Operational Discipline focus — establish clear ownership, governance and execution cadence.

ADKAR Focus (Awareness/Desire trigger):
• Awareness: clarify where governance gaps create delay and risk.
• Desire: align to a single operating cadence and decision forum.
• Knowledge: define owners, decision rights, and escalation paths.
• Ability: implement a lightweight operating rhythm.
• Reinforcement: review KPIs and decisions consistently in QBRs.
Low.recommendedPrograms:
Workflow Clarity – Recommended OCM Tactics:
• Operating Rhythm Design
• Decision Rights + Escalation Paths
• Commitment Tracking System
• Process Stabilization Workshops
• KPI Scorecards in Governance
